i feel calm, but i also feel bothered by the table, the branch, and the melting clocks. my eyes constantly stare at the ocean and the cliff because they remind me of freedom, whereas the clocks and shadows exist as constraints, which subconsciously remind me that freedom is not free. the poor tree branch also tells me that no one is immortal in this cruel world. gyubee japanese bbq. enjoyed the unlimited beef glitch (happiness) until the 2-hour limit is up (melting clocks, reality). lost in yesterday by tame impala. used: vintage-style synth (by gpt): reminiscent feeling bass: don't think too much, enjoy the moment. the soundtrack evokes a sense of nostalgia, creating a stress-free environment. bathroom by yourself fall

this painting evokes a sense of unease and contemplation. the strange, melting objects create a dreamlike atmosphere that feels both fascinating and slightly disturbing. it makes me feel like time itself is bending and becoming unreliable, which creates a reflective mood about the nature of reality and perception. melted cheese on toast - something that's transformed from its original form into something droopy and soft, with warm golden colors that mirror the painting's palette. a slow, ambient piece with echoing piano notes that seem to stretch and distort. occasional deep, resonant tones create a sense of vast emptiness, while gentle synthesizer waves suggest something gradually softening and dissolving. the tempo would be unhurried, almost as if time itself is moving differently. living room friends summer

nostalgic, wistful, scattered white bread muted tremolo in the cello and double basses section sparsely interjected by rapid, restless atonal figures on an oboe; harmonically ambiguous with unsettled meter; feels fleeting and ambiguous, fragmented office by yourself,strangers winter

the clocks being dried out all over a desert dry area makes it seem like time is scarce and dry in the world; it means to valuable time and how limited time is for our lives. it shows how time is fluid, and how time always passes, but also leaves memories with us; memory fades and turns into a soup of ideas, which shows the strange assortment of things around in the environment of the drawing. it would be something like my mother's cooking, like dumplings or baozi, something persistent throughout my life and hold fond memories for me, but also quite blurry given how many times i've consumed that food. to me, it reminds me of "a burning memory," which was an experimental album meant to simulate dementia. to me, it follows similar themes in long nostalgic, yet not fully known melodies meant to poke at the brain in terms of memories which are fuzzy and not fully clear due to the passage of time (and for the album, simulation of dementia). bathroom by yourself winter

this painting fills me with a sense of wonder and awe. the swirling movement in the sky creates a feeling of energy and dynamism, almost as if the universe is alive and breathing. it's both peaceful and exhilarating at the same time - the quiet village below contrasts beautifully with the turbulent, magical sky above. blueberry pie with whipped cream and a dusting of golden sugar stars - rich, deep blue swirls with bright yellow accents, both comforting and magical. a sweeping orchestral piece that builds from soft, peaceful strings into a magnificent crescendo. violins swirl upward in spiraling phrases, with bright brass punctuating moments of wonder. the music has a sense of movement and energy, yet underlying it all is a gentle, comforting melody that evokes quiet contemplation of something vast and beautiful. bedroom family members winter
